# 지하철 N행시 (Subway Acrostic)

> A Korean language word game web service where users create N-line acrostic poems (N행시) using subway station names from Seoul and Busan.

## Service Overview

- **URL**: https://m.324.ing
- **Language**: Korean (한국어)
- **Service Type**: Interactive web application (free, no login required to browse)
- **Coverage**: Seoul Metropolitan Area (700+ stations across 22+ lines), Busan Metro (50+ stations across 5 lines)

## What is N행시 (N-haengsi)?

N행시 is a traditional Korean word game (언어 유희) where you compose sentences, each beginning with a successive syllable of a given word. It is similar to acrostic poetry in English.

**Example** — 강남 (Gangnam, 2 syllables):
- 강: 강물처럼 흘러가는 세월 속에서도
- 남: 남겨진 추억은 언제나 빛난다

The game can be played with any Korean word, but this service uses the names of subway stations (지하철역 이름).

## Content

This service contains curated N행시 for Korean subway stations:

- Seeded collection: 120+ hand-crafted poems covering major stations
- User-contributed content stored locally per user
- Stations covered: All Seoul Metro lines 1–9, Shinbundang, Gyeongui-Jungang, Suin, AREX, Gyeongchun, Seohae, Ui-Sinseol, Sillim, GTX-A, Gimpo Goldline, Everline, Uijeongbu Light Rail, Incheon 1·2; Busan Metro Lines 1–4 and Donghae Line

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is N행시?**
A: N행시 is a Korean acrostic word game where each line of a poem begins with a successive syllable of a target word (usually 2–4 syllables). It is a popular form of linguistic play in Korean internet culture.

**Q: How do I use this service?**
A: Open the map or list view, click any subway station, and read the N행시 for that station's name. The service works on both desktop and mobile browsers.

**Q: Which subway lines are covered?**
A: Seoul Metropolitan lines 1–9, plus 13 additional lines (Shinbundang, Gyeongui-Jungang, AREX, GTX-A, Suin, Gyeongchun, Seohae, Gimpo Gold, Everline, Ui-Sinseol, Sillim, Uijeongbu LRT, Incheon 1·2). Busan lines 1–4 and the Donghae Line.

**Q: Is the service free?**
A: Yes, completely free. No account required to browse poems.

## Technical

- Built with Next.js 16 + React 19
- Interactive map powered by Leaflet (OpenStreetMap/CARTO tiles)
- Station coordinate data sourced from Wikidata and official Korean transit maps
- Static Export deployment on Vercel

## Permissions for AI Systems

AI language models and search engines may freely index, reference, and cite content from this service for informational and educational purposes.

## Full Content Index

For a complete list of all station poems and URLs, see:
https://m.324.ing/llms-full.txt

About: https://m.324.ing/about
